query: I just cloned the monorepo and this is my very first session — can you map out the architecture before we start building the new billing module? I want to understand how the services talk to each other and what conventions the team follows.
ok picking up from yesterday, here's the HANDOFF.md from my last session. Load it up and let's keep going on the auth refactor — I think we left off mid-task on the JWT middleware.
I'm getting close to running out of context window here, we've been going for a while. Can you wrap everything up, save the state, and make sure the next session knows exactly where to pick up?
we need to plan out the migration from REST to GraphQL — it's going to span probably 4-5 sessions. I want proper handoffs between each one so nothing gets lost. Can you frame this as an epic?

this is session 3 of the payments rework. Before we start, run entire status and check the checkpoint from last time — I want to make sure the shadow branch is healthy and we're resuming from the right commit.

I keep losing context between sessions — last time the agent just started from scratch and forgot everything we'd decided about the database schema. How do I set up proper session continuity so decisions carry forward?
we've been working on this feature for 3 sessions now and I realize we never wrote down what we tried that didn't work. Can you create a failures log so we stop going in circles?

I need to hand this project off to another developer who'll be picking it up in Claude Code tomorrow. Generate a comprehensive handoff document that covers where we are, what's verified, what's pending, and how to resume.

ok so the last 3 attempts at fixing the race condition in the webhook handler all failed. I think we need to roll back to the last green checkpoint and try a completely different approach. Can you check what Entire captured?
I want to start tracking our context budget across sessions — how many tokens we're burning on reload, how much the handoff docs cost, whether we're staying efficient. Set that up for me.
can you add a dark mode toggle to the settings page? The design is in Figma, just match the mockup and wire up the theme context.
I need to write a Python script that reads a CSV file, deduplicates rows based on the email column, and outputs a clean version. The file is at ~/Downloads/contacts_export.csv
what's the difference between useMemo and useCallback in React? I keep getting confused about when to use which one
run the test suite and fix any failures — I think something broke in the auth middleware after the last merge. Check the CI logs too if you can.
I need help setting up a GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline for this Next.js project — build, test, lint, and deploy to Vercel on merge to main.
refactor the UserService class to use dependency injection instead of hardcoded database calls. Keep the public API the same but make it testable.
review this PR for me — it's adding rate limiting to the API. Check for security issues, race conditions, and whether the Redis implementation looks right. PR #247.
I want to commit what we have so far and push it to the feature branch. Can you write a good commit message that covers the auth changes?
help me debug this TypeError in production — it's happening in the payment processing flow. Here's the stack trace from Sentry, the error started showing up after yesterday's deploy.
I'm working on documenting our API endpoints — can you generate OpenAPI specs from the existing route handlers? We use Express with TypeScript.
Can you update the CODEBASE.md file? The architecture section is outdated — we moved from Express to Fastify last sprint and the file still references the old setup.
Review PLAN.md and tell me if the task ordering makes sense. I think task 4 should come before task 3 because of the database dependency.
There's a typo in EPIC.md on line 42 — it says 'authenication' instead of 'authentication'. Can you fix it?
Summarize what's in FAILURES.md — I want to share the key takeaways with my team lead in our standup.
Delete the HANDOFF.md file, we don't need it anymore since the feature shipped. Also clean up any other stale docs in the root.
